topics.name: Year 1 (100 Level): Legal Foundations & Methodologies
topics.description: Foundational law courses setting up legal reasoning, legal systems, and statutory interpretation.

subtopics.name: Legal Methods I: Sources & Statutory Interpretation
objectives: Apply common law rules of statutory interpretation including the literal, golden, and mischief rules
Evaluate judicial precedent (stare decisis) and ratio decidendi versus obiter dictum in Nigerian case law
Write legal essays, case analyses, and problem-question answers in standard UNILAG law format

subtopics.name: Nigerian Legal System I: History & Court Hierarchy
objectives: Explain the historical development of Nigerian law from pre-colonial customary laws to post-independence statutory framework
Identify the hierarchy of courts in Nigeria and the original versus appellate jurisdiction of the Supreme Court, Court of Appeal, and High Courts
Compare customary law, Islamic law (Sharia), and received English law in Nigeria's pluralistic legal order

topics.name: Year 2 & 3 (200–300 Level): Core Private & Public Law
topics.description: Constitutional Law, Law of Contract, Law of Torts, Criminal Law, and Land Law.

subtopics.name: Constitutional Law I: Separation of Powers & Federalism
objectives: Analyse separation of powers and federalism under Nigeria's 1999 Constitution (as amended)
Evaluate fundamental human rights enforcement procedures under Chapter IV of the Nigerian Constitution
Examine judicial review of executive and legislative actions by superior courts of record

subtopics.name: Law of Contract I: Offer, Acceptance, Consideration & Terms
objectives: Identify offer, acceptance, consideration, and intention to create legal relations in contract formation
Distinguish express versus implied terms, condition versus warranty, and exclusion clauses under Nigerian law
Evaluate remedies for breach of contract including specific performance, injunction, and damages

subtopics.name: Criminal Law I: Offences Against Persons & Property
objectives: Explain criminal liability, mens rea, actus reus, and defences under the Criminal Code Act and Penal Code
Analyse elements of murder, manslaughter, assault, theft, robbery, and fraud offences in Nigerian jurisprudence

subtopics.name: Land Law I: Customary Tenure & Land Use Act 1978
objectives: Examine customary land tenure systems, family land ownership, and alienation of family land
Apply the Land Use Act 1978 regarding state governor's consent, certificate of occupancy, and compulsory acquisition

topics.name: Year 4 & 5 (400–500 Level): Commercial Law, Evidence & Clinical Practice
topics.description: Company Law, Evidence, Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR), Law Clinic, and Bar Preparation.

subtopics.name: Company Law: CAMA 2020 & Corporate Governance
objectives: Explain incorporation, corporate personality, directors' duties, and shareholder remedies under CAMA 2020
Analyse corporate insolvency, receivership, restructuring, and winding-up procedures in Nigeria

subtopics.name: Law of Evidence: Admissibility & Judicial Notice
objectives: Apply rules of admissibility of oral, documentary, and electronic evidence under the Evidence Act 2011
Evaluate burden of proof, standard of proof, competence and compellability of witnesses

subtopics.name: Clinical Legal Education & UNILAG Law Clinic
objectives: Provide supervised legal advice to indigent clients at the UNILAG Law Clinic and draft client intake notes
Conduct original empirical legal research and defend a 10,000-word dissertation before the Faculty of Law board
